event: transported to our hospital by ambulance
Japan Coma Scale (JCS) score was II-10
no clear neurological deficits were observed
non-contrast head CT revealed hemorrhage in the third and fourth ventricles, and bilateral lateral ventricles with left dominance
hemorrhage was confined within the ventricles
in the arterial phase, the source of bleeding was unclear
spot enhancement on the lateral wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
blood pressure control was initiated in the intensive care unit
cerebral angiograph revealed an aneurysm (3.8 mm in size) at the distal site of the mLSA arising from the proximal A1 segment of the left ACA
considered to be a ruptured aneurysm
guiding sheath was navigated into the petrous portion of the left internal carotid artery
compression study of the right common carotid artery confirmed the anterior communicating artery (AComA)
Marathon catheter was advanced through the AComA into the mLSA origin, but was unable to be advanced to a site near the aneurysm
changed to a combination of a DeFrictor nano catheter and TENROU S10 guide wire
selective angiography confirmed the location of the catheter tip proximal to the aneurysm
perforating artery distal and proximal to the aneurysm were confirmed to be filled with NBCA
angiography after NBCA injection demonstrated disappearance of the aneurysm
slight reflux of NBCA was observed in the proximal artery
no signs of hemorrhagic complications on the postoperative head CT
no cerebral infarction on the postoperative head CT
Lipiodol remained as a high-density spot on the lateral ventricular wall of the anterior horn of the left lateral ventricle
MRI evaluation was not possible due to biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
decrease in muscle strength associated with disuse remained
implantation of a biventricular pacing implantable cardioverter defibrillator
not accompanied by cerebral parenchymal hemorrhage
